item.title: Philippine Peso Drops Past Key 60-Per-Dollar Level on Oil Risk
item.description: The Philippine peso fell past the key level of 60 per dollar as elevated oil prices weigh on the country’s economic outlook.

item.title: Chinese Funds Favor Petchems Over Metals as War Roils Futures
item.description: Chinese investors are favoring wagers on petrochemicals over base metals, as they trade the fallout from the war in Iran on local futures markets.

item.title: Philippine Wealth Fund Likes Physical Assets as Iran War Rages
item.description: The Philippines’ sovereign wealth fund says investing in physical assets like metals makes increasing sense against the backdrop of a war in Iran that’s playing havoc with the nation’s fuel market.

item.title: JPMorgan Shut China Tycoon’s Account That Made Millions for Bank
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. has shut the private banking account of Tang Hao, a Chinese investor who rose to prominence after taking a multibillion-dollar stake in one of the world’s top-performing stocks, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Australia Appoints Fuel Czar to Tackle Iran War Disruptions
item.description: Australia appointed former energy regulator Anthea Harris as a new fuel czar to coordinate the response to price spikes and supply chain disruptions as a result of the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Bolivia Wins Credit Rating Upgrade After Coupon Payment Pledge
item.description: Moody’s Ratings lifted Bolivia’s credit rating following a pledge by the government it will be able to meet dollar bond coupon payments this month.

item.title: Australian Hiring Stays Strong, Backing Hawkish RBA Stance
item.description: Australian unemployment stayed relatively low in February as job gains continued, reinforcing the Reserve Bank’s view that the economy remains resilient enough to withstand tighter monetary policy.

item.title: Mexico REIT Fibra Macquarie Set for Bidding War After New Offers
item.description: Two Mexican real estate investment trusts have said they plan to bid for peer Fibra Macquarie, one day after Fibra Prologis shareholders signed off on plans to buy it.

item.title: RBA Warns of Global Financial Stability Risks as Iran War Rages
item.description: Australia’s central bank said local households and firms are broadly well placed to cope with recent interest-rate increases and surging fuel prices from the escalating Middle East conflict, while highlighting the potential for an unexpected shock as a result of elevated international risk.

item.title: Japan Stocks Fall After Oil Price Surges, Fed Holds Key Rate
item.description: Japanese stocks fell after oil prices surged again with new attacks on Middle East energy infrastructure and the US Federal Reserve said it won’t cut interest rates again until inflation resumes cooling.

item.title: ECB to Hold as It Weighs War’s Inflation Threat: Decision Guide
item.description: The European Central Bank is set to keep interest rates unchanged as it gauges how much of an inflation shock the war in Iran will deliver.

item.title: US Tells Companies to Secure Microsoft System After Stryker Hack
item.description: The US government is warning businesses to secure their corporate accounts within a popular Microsoft Corp. management tool, following a cyberattack on Stryker Corp. last week.

item.title: UK to Hike Steel Tariffs, Cut Import Quotas to Boost Industry
item.description: The UK government said it will hike tariffs on steel imports and cut import quotas, as it seeks to boost the country’s ailing domestic steel industry amid fierce global competition.

item.title: Iran War Puts Bank of England in Holding Pattern: Decision Guide
item.description: The Bank of England is set to skip an interest-rate cut markets once saw as a near certainty, as officials pause to assess the disruption unleashed by conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Oil-Driven Inflation Fears Are Reshaping Asian Bond Yield Curves
item.description: A reset in some of emerging Asia’s bond yield curves may be imminent as analysts signal an end to the recent surge in borrowing costs.

item.title: Pop Mart Partners Sony for Labubu Movie to Revive Fading Craze
item.description: Pop Mart International Group Ltd. is bringing Labubu to the big screen, betting that a cinematic storyline can revive interest in the quirky toy as sales growth slows and investor enthusiasm wanes.

item.title: China Rate Markets Signal Cooling Bets on Deflation, PBOC Easing
item.description: China’s interest rate markets are indicating reduced expectations for further easing of funding conditions and monetary policy, reflecting renewed economic optimism and lingering concerns about elevated oil prices.

item.title: Brazil’s Modest Rate Cut Seen Supporting Real, Easing Yields
item.description: Brazil’s cautious start to interest-rate cuts is expected to support local assets, underpinning the currency and easing pressure on short-term yields, money managers say.

item.title: Yen Nears 160 Per Dollar, Japanese Stocks Fall Ahead of BOJ
item.description: Investors are on alert for the yen’s potential breach of 160 against the dollar, while Japanese stocks and government bond futures declined, ahead of the Bank of Japan’s rate decision announcement on Thursday.

item.title: AI Is Being Built to Replace You—Not Help You
item.description: Nobel Prize–winning economist Daron Acemoglu warns that multiple factors are driving a labor-replacement model, with potentially dire consequences for society.

item.title: Oil Jumps as Iran and Israel Trade Strikes on Energy Facilities
Meeting Surging Demand for AI Memory Chips Has a Climate Cost
item.description: The rush to boost production of memory chips to meet fast accelerating demand from artificial intelligence will add to the semiconductor sector’s climate footprint and risks lifting costs of managing emissions.

item.title: China Luxury EV Maker Voyah Set for HK Debut Without Fresh Funds
item.description: Voyah Automobile Technology Co., the luxury electric vehicles arm of Dongfeng Motor Group, begins trading in Hong Kong on Thursday after a listing that didn’t involve selling new shares or raising funds.

item.title: Wells Fargo Is Said to Hire UBS’s Keller to Lead M&A Structuring
item.description: Wells Fargo & Co. has hired Derek Keller from UBS Group AG as a managing director and head of M&A structuring, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Japan Stock Traders’ Guide to Navigating Takaichi-Trump Summit
item.description: Japan’s equity investors will closely watch a meeting between President Donald Trump and Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi in Washington on Thursday for possible agreements on economic and military cooperation.

item.title: Gold Steadies After Six-Day Decline as Fed Warns on Inflation
item.description: Gold steadied, after tumbling nearly 4% in the previous session, with the Federal Reserve warning of inflation risks tied to the Middle East war and surging energy prices.

item.title: Asian Stocks to Fall as Iran War Saps Sentiment: Markets Wrap
item.description: Asian equities faced early declines Thursday as signs of escalating tensions in the Middle East drove oil higher and weighed on US stocks and bonds.

item.title: Powell Says War Too Soon to Judge as Inflation Keeps Fed on Hold
item.description: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell made it clear the US central bank won’t cut interest rates again until inflation resumes cooling. And that’s before it even starts considering the possible impact of the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Oil Advances After Iran, Israel Strike Major Energy Assets
item.description: Oil rose after Iran and Israel traded strikes on key energy facilities in the Middle East, boosting turmoil in markets as the conflict stretched toward three weeks.

item.title: Quebec Projects Smaller Deficit, Aims to Balance Budget by 2030
item.description: The Canadian province of Quebec says it will trim its budget deficit in the years ahead as it brings in more tax revenue and restrains new spending, despite trade tensions with the US.
